description: Migration up creates user_cost_rates with expected columns, PK (tenant, rate_id), and index (tenant, user_id, effective_from); down drops it
user_cost_rates rejects negative cost_rate and effective_to < effective_from at the DB layer (CHECK constraints)
Model-layer referential integrity (no DB FK on Citus): upsertCostRate rejects a rate for a nonexistent or cross-tenant user_id; NULL user_id (tenant default) succeeds
invoice_time_entries.item_id migration adds nullable column; existing rows unaffected
Model insert/list/update/delete round-trip for user cost rates, tenant-scoped (rows from another tenant invisible)
Overlap validation rejects a new row overlapping an existing closed range for the same user
Overlap validation rejects a new row overlapping an existing open-ended (effective_to NULL) range
Overlap validation allows adjacent ranges (prev effective_to = 2026-03-31, next effective_from = 2026-04-01)
Overlap validation scopes by user: same range for a different user (or the tenant default) is allowed
Rate resolution picks user-specific rate over tenant default when both cover the work date
Rate resolution falls back to tenant default when no user row covers the date
Rate resolution returns NULL (uncosted) when neither user nor default covers the date
Rate resolution respects effective boundaries: work on effective_from date uses the new rate; the day before uses the old rate
Rate resolution joins on work_date exclusively (NOT NULL by schema): an entry whose start_time falls on a different calendar day than its work_date costs per the work_date rate
listCostRates requires billing.read: user without it gets permission error
upsertCostRate/deleteCostRate require billing.update: read-only billing user is rejected
upsertCostRate surfaces overlap error as a typed, user-displayable validation failure (not a 500)
covers-worked-time indicator true when the edited range intersects existing time entries for that user, false otherwise
listCostRates returns only internal users and is tenant-scoped
Cost Rates screen renders user list with current rates and default banner (component test)
Add-rate dialog: currency input stores cents (e.g. entering 62.50 persists 6250)
Editing a rate whose range covers worked time shows the warning dialog before saving
Empty state shown when tenant has zero cost-rate rows; links to add default
Cost Rates screen has kebab-case ids on buttons/dialogs/inputs and no hardcoded strings
Invoice generation from ticket time entries writes invoice_time_entries rows with the correct item_id for a 1-entry:1-item invoice
Invoice generation from 3 time entries produces 3 invoice_charges rows (1:1) with 3 distinct item_ids on their link rows
Manual invoice generation unaffected: no invoice_time_entries rows, no errors
Regression: existing invoice generation integration tests still pass with the item_id change
Revenue CTE counts sent/paid/partially_applied/overdue plus legacy open/completed/Unpaid invoices, and excludes draft/cancelled/pending/prepayment
Revenue CTE uses allocated_amount from fixed details when present and net_amount otherwise, without double counting
Discount line items (negative net_amount) reduce revenue in all groupings
Tax is excluded from revenue (net_amount, not total with tax)
Foreign-currency invoice amounts convert via exchange_rate_basis_points into tenant currency
Labor cost = round(actual minutes x rate / 60) per entry; verified against hand-computed fixture including odd minutes rounding
Non-billable time (billable_duration = 0) still accrues labor cost and hours
Zero/negative-duration entries (end_time <= start_time) are clamped to 0 cost minutes and reported in zeroDurationEntryCount
Uncosted entries contribute minutes but zero cost, and uncostedMinutes is reported at summary, client, and agreement levels
Material revenue and cost computed from ticket_materials quantity x rate and quantity x service_catalog.cost
project_materials included in client totals; at agreement grain material revenue lands in the Ad-hoc row and material cost in the Unattributed row (no contract mapping exists for material charges)
Material rows with currency_code different from tenant currency are flagged and excluded from converted totals
getProfitabilitySummary returns correct totals for a seeded fixture with known revenue/cost
Summary EHR = revenue / actual hours; matches hand-computed fixture
getClientProfitability groups by client and matches per-client fixture expectations

'No client' row appears when ad_hoc / non_billable_category time exists in range (tickets.client_id is NOT NULL on cleanly migrated DBs, so the row is driven by clientless work-item types) and disappears otherwise
getAgreementProfitability revenue attribution uses invoice_charges.client_contract_id
Agreement cost/hours attribution: time entries route via contract_line_id -> contract_lines -> contracts -> client_contracts (contract_id join; client_contract_lines is dropped)
Ad-hoc/manual row aggregates revenue with NULL client_contract_id per client
Unattributed row aggregates time with NULL contract_line_id per client
Reconciliation: agreement rows + ad-hoc + unattributed sum to the client row for revenue, cost, and minutes
Per-contract-line breakdown sums to its agreement row
EHR at client and agreement level null-safe when hours are zero (no division by zero, renders as em dash)
getTicketProfitability returns one row per ticket with minutes, billable minutes, labor cost, material cost, revenue, margin
Ticket filter by clientId and clientContractId narrows rows correctly
Hourly ticket revenue exact path: item_id-linked entries produce revenue equal to invoice line amounts, tagged exact
Defensive N:1 handling: if multiple link rows share an item_id, by-billable_duration apportionment sums exactly to the item amount with no double counting
Legacy invoices (item_id NULL): revenue stays at agreement level as a tagged residual; no per-ticket revenue fabricated; agreement and client totals still reconcile

Fixed-line allocation: single fixed charge (with detail service period) spread across 3 tickets proportional to actual hours on the detail's contract line; allocations sum exactly to the charge (largest-remainder)
Fixed-line allocation with zero ticket hours in the period: charge revenue lands in an agreement-level residual, not lost or NaN

Bucket-line allocation: overage charges spread proportionally by hours, tagged allocated; fixture seeds the bucket base fee as a fixed charge (matching the engine, which emits overage-only bucket charges) and verifies it allocates under the fixed rule
Ticket materials revenue/cost included per ticket via billed_invoice_id and quantity x cost
Ticket view excludes project_task time entries
Multi-tenant isolation: identical fixtures in two tenants; each tenant's report contains only its own data (all four actions)
Code review check: every new query includes tenant in WHERE and JOIN conditions (manual checklist item on PR)
All four report actions reject users lacking billing.read
Date-range boundaries: invoice on end date included, day after excluded; work_date on start date included, day before excluded
Timing split: work in month 1, invoice in month 2 — month-1 report shows cost only, month-2 shows revenue only
Empty range returns zero-valued summary and empty tables without errors
Effective-date boundary within range: user rate changes mid-range; cost uses old rate before and new rate after the boundary (acceptance criterion 2)
ProfitabilityReport renders summary cards from getProfitabilitySummary (component test with mocked actions)
Date-range picker defaults to the last complete month and refetches on change
Client table sorts by margin and margin %; clicking a client drills into agreements
Agreement table shows Ad-hoc and Unattributed rows and expands to contract lines
Ticket table shows attribution tag (exact/allocated) and uncosted indicator
Timing-basis tooltip present on the summary header
'Cost rates not configured' empty state shown when tenant has no rates; links to settings
Uncosted-minutes, unapproved-minutes, zero-duration, uncosted-materials, and unconverted-revenue warnings each render when the actions report nonzero counts
Error state renders on action failure consistent with sibling tabs
getProfitabilityReport removed: no remaining references compile; Profitability tab uses new component only
contracts.profitability absent from ReportRegistry; build/typecheck passes and no references to the removed definition remain
i18n validation script passes with new keys in all language packs (generate-pseudo-locales + validate-translations run)
npm run build passes for server and affected packages
Fixture-based end-to-end scenario: seed client with hourly agreement + fixed agreement + materials + mixed rates; verify every acceptance criterion 1-6 number matches hand-computed spreadsheet
Report with cost rates configured but zero time entries: margin equals revenue, EHR shown as em dash
Large-fixture smoke: 10k time entries / 1k invoices in range completes within acceptable latency (baseline measurement recorded in scratchpad)
Fixed-charge allocation window fallback: charge without detail service period uses invoices.billing_period_start/end and its ticket rows are tagged 'allocated (approximate)'
Fixed charge without any detail rows stays at agreement level (unassigned-to-line residual); agreement totals still reconcile
Foreign-currency invoice with NULL exchange_rate_basis_points excluded from converted totals and counted in the unconverted-revenue warning
Unbilled material contributes cost only, never revenue; billed material revenue dated by invoice_date and filtered by countable status
Billed material whose service has NULL service_catalog.cost is flagged as uncosted material, not treated as zero cost

Multiple client_contracts rows for the same contract_id (junction-table possibility; zero in production today): entry cost lands in exactly one agreement (date range covering work_date wins, deterministic tiebreak); no double counting
Per-line revenue attribution via invoice_charge_details.config_id -> contract_line_service_configuration.contract_line_id matches fixture expectations

Legacy invoice mixing time-based and fixed charges (NULL item_id): the entire invoice's revenue remains at agreement level; its tickets show labor cost with zero attributed revenue and the agreement residual carries the revenue
Fixed charge with no detail period and NULL invoices.billing_period_start/end lands in the agreement-level residual (terminal rule); reconciliation holds
Time entry on a contract line whose contract has no covering client_contracts row: cost folds into the client's Unattributed row; agreement rows still reconcile to the client row
Interaction time entries attribute cost to the interaction's client; appointment_request time to its client (client-attribution chain, not just ticket+project)
Concurrent upsertCostRate calls for the same (tenant, user) with overlapping ranges: advisory lock serializes them and exactly one succeeds
With a seeded overlapping rate pair (validation bypassed), resolution still returns exactly one rate per entry and labor cost is not double-counted
Unapproved (DRAFT/submitted) time accrues cost and is counted in unapprovedMinutes; approved time is not counted there
Migration on a Citus cluster: user_cost_rates creates and distributes successfully (bare table, guarded distribute, transaction:false, no FK failures)
